texto: Poetry offers works of art that are beautiful, like paintings, which are my second favorite work of the art, but there are also works of art that embody emotion and that are kind of school for feeling. They teach how to feel, and they do this by the means of their beauty of language.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Donald Hall: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a educacao. O trecho “Poetry offers works of art that are beautiful, like paintings, which are my second favorite work …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, work, art pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e art.

texto: I loathe the trivialization of poetry that happens in creative writing classes. Teachers set exercises to stimulate subject matter: Write a poem about an imaginary landscape with real people in it. Write about a place your parents lived in before you were born. We have enough terrible poetry around without encouraging more of it.

texto: I have written some poetry and two prose books about baseball, but if I had been a rich man, I probably would not have written many of the magazine essays that I have had to do. texto: I would work until I got stuck, and I would put it down and pick up something else. I might be able to take a 20-minute nap and get to work again. That way, I was able to work about 10 hours a day... It was important to me to work every day. I managed to work on Christmas day, just to be able to say I worked 365 days a year.
